title: Buttondown vs AWeber (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Buttondown and AWeber: pricing, features, and ease of use to find the best email marketing tool for your needs.
intro: Both Buttondown and AWeber are established email marketing platforms offering free tiers and affordable paid plans. Buttondown appeals to technical users and writers who value markdown editing and API access, while AWeber caters to bloggers and solopreneurs seeking simplicity and built-in landing pages. Your choice depends on whether you prioritize developer-friendly features or user-friendly automation.
choose_a_if: You're a developer or technical writer comfortable with markdown formatting
You need API access to integrate with custom tools or build automation
You want the lowest entry price for paid plans at $9/month
choose_b_if: You're a beginner looking for an intuitive, visual editor and templates
You need landing pages to build your email list without additional tools
You want a larger free tier with up to 500 subscribers before paying

faq.a: It depends on your needs. Buttondown excels for developers and markdown enthusiasts with strong API support, while AWeber is better for beginners and non-technical users who want simplicity. Buttondown has higher customer satisfaction ratings (4.8 vs 4.2 on G2), but AWeber offers more traditional email marketing features like landing pages and autoresponders.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Buttondown or AWeber?
faq.a: Buttondown is cheaper for paid plans at $9/month versus AWeber's $15/month. However, AWeber's free tier is more generous with 500 subscribers compared to Buttondown's 100, making AWeber better if you want to stay free longer. For most users, Buttondown becomes the budget option once you upgrade.

faq.q: Can I switch from AWeber to Buttondown?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support import/export functionality. Buttondown explicitly lists import/export as a key feature, allowing you to migrate subscriber lists and data. The process typically involves exporting your contacts as CSV from AWeber and importing them into Buttondown, though you may lose some advanced AWeber-specific features like landing page data.
